summary: Full backend + frontend testing for QuantEdge algo crypto trading dashboard. 16/16 backend pytest cases pass (health, market data, signals with Claude AI, portfolio, manual trades, bot start/stop, metrics). Frontend dashboard renders fully with TopBar, MarketStrip, PriceChart, BotControl strategies, AIInsights (HOLD/72% confidence with full reasoning + key factors), LiveSignals, TradeHistory, PortfolioPanel, ManualTradePanel. Manual BUY, ticker switching, AI signal generation (~30s), bot start/stop, and portfolio reset all work end-to-end against the public preview URL.

minor.issue: Filters closed trades by type in ('CLOSE','STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT') only — manual SELL trades use type='MANUAL', so they are NOT counted in win_rate_pct / total_pnl / total_trades. Either include 'MANUAL' SELL (side=='SELL') in closed-trade filter, or tag manual closes with type='CLOSE'.

Returned trade has entry_price set to current price (matches BUY because same-second test). Code in server.py line 280 passes entry_price=pos['entry_price'] correctly, but pnl came back as 0.0 because price didn't move — expected, no bug.

issues: Recharts console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fires 4x on initial render — ResponsiveContainer parent likely has 0 height before layout settles. Cosmetic, chart renders correctly after.

action_items: Update /api/metrics in server.py to also count manual SELL trades (or change _log_trade to set type='CLOSE' for manual sells with non-zero pnl) so win_rate_pct, total_pnl, total_trades reflect manual paper trades.
Optional: silence recharts width/height warning by giving chart container an explicit min-height while data loads.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/signals/{symbol} action-resolution ternary (line 122) is hard to read; extract to a helper that picks BUY/SELL/HOLD between classical and AI per documented logic.
server.py: ObjectId is properly excluded via projection {'_id':0} on list endpoints and pop on /signals — good.
server.py: bot_status endpoint relies on bot.status() being sync — verify under load that bot.config dict mutation in /bot/config is thread-safe with the running loop.
Metrics ignore manual SELL closures (see backend minor).
Frontend uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly, all interactive controls have data-testid as required.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is read at call time inside ai_signals._get_key — good for hot reload.
seed_data_creation: Portfolio reset to $10,000 via POST /api/portfolio/reset; BUY 500 USD ETHUSDT then SELL same; one BTCUSDT $500 manual BUY left open after frontend run (recommend reset before next iteration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the source of truth (16 tests, ~18s). Trade documents use field 'side' for BUY/SELL and 'type' for source ('MANUAL','BOT','CLOSE','STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT'). AI signal endpoint takes ~25-40s — keep timeout >=60s. After tests run, an open BTCUSDT position remains in paper portfolio; call POST /api/portfolio/reset to clean up. Frontend stop-bot-btn only mounts when bot is running (toggle render), not a bug.
rca of the issue: Metrics-vs-manual-trades RCA: /api/metrics line 216 in server.py filters closed = [t for t in trades if t['type'] in ('CLOSE','STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT')]. _log_trade in bot.py records manual sells with type='MANUAL' (passed from server.py line 280). Result: any manual paper-trade round-trip never increments wins/losses/total_pnl. Mitigation: either change filter to include side=='SELL' with non-zero pnl, or in /api/trades/manual SELL branch pass source='CLOSE' to _log_trade.
